deliverables.title: Proposal
deliverables.description: Outline the recommended approach, scope, timeline, and cost for potential engagement.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Intake Agent harness reads SOW, BD estimate, and discovery inputs → extracts structured `scope.md` and `constraints.md` committed to Git (Layer 1). Proposal narrative sections are generated from these structured files via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork). Human PM and Client Partner review and approve before client delivery. PDF is a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Define scope, delivery approach
- Estimate effort, timeline and project fees
- Document assumptions and exclusions

deliverables.title: SOW / CO
deliverables.description: Formally document agreed scope, pricing, and contractual changes.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Human-owned contractual document. Post-signature, Intake Agent harness reads the signed SOW → commits `brief.md`, `scope.md`, and `constraints.md` to Git (Layer 1) as the machine-readable SOW summary consumed by all downstream agent harnesses. This structured intake is the intelligence substrate for the entire engagement.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Document scope, deliverables and pricing
- Align on assumptions and dependencies
- Support review and signature process

deliverables.title: Internal Kick Off
deliverables.description: Align the internal team on scope, roles, risks, and delivery approach before engaging the client.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Kickoff deck is generated from `brief.md`, `scope.md`, and `constraints.md` (Layer 1, Git). Agent harness drafts narrative sections — project goals, scope summary, risk identification — from structured intake inputs. Human PM reviews and refines via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork). PDF is a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Translate all internal inputs into kick off deck narrative, outlining why we're working on this, project goals, scope and success criteria
- Define working dynamics and team's expectations
- Identify initial risks and dependencies

deliverables.title: External Kick Off
deliverables.description: Establish shared understanding of goals, scope, approach, and next steps with the client.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Generated from Git-backed structured inputs — scope, goals, and delivery approach are drawn directly from `brief.md` and `scope.md` (Layer 1). Agent harness produces first-draft deck from internal kickoff content. Human PM reviews and approves (Layer 2 gate). PDF is a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Define scope, goals, and delivery approach
- Confirm roles, cadence and communication with client team
- Align on success metrics and immediate next steps

deliverables.title: RACI
deliverables.description: Clear definition of roles and responsibilities to support efficient decision-making and execution.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent harness generates RACI from `brief.md` scope definitions and role configurations (Layer 1, Git). `raci.md` is committed to Git as the canonical version — updated by harness when scope changes are committed. External PDF is a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Define roles and responsibilities by deliverable.
- Confirm decision-makers and approvers.
- Validate engagement expectations across team.
- Update RACI as scope or team composition changes.

deliverables.title: RAIDD Log
deliverables.description: Track risks, assumptions, issues, dependencies, and decisions throughout delivery.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `raidd.md` in Git (Layer 1) is the canonical source. Agent harness monitors delivery signals — PR blockers, missed milestones, open decisions — and flags new RAIDD items for PM review. Smartsheet/Notion views are Layer 3 generated outputs, not the source of truth. Human PM owns item triage and mitigation ownership.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Log and maintain RAIDD items
- Define owners and mitigation plans
- Review regularly with team and client

humanGate.action: Triages agent-flagged RAIDD items, assigns mitigation owners, and closes resolved items
humanGate.trigger: Ongoing — each time agent flags a new item

deliverables.title: Account Delivery Update
deliverables.description: Regular internal view of delivery health, risks, and financials across the account.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Delivery health summary is auto-generated from Git-native metrics: DORA indicators, milestone progress, open RAIDD items, and sprint velocity (Tier 2/3 Observability). Agent harness synthesizes into structured report and posts to Slack or Notion. Human PM/ALT reviews before circulation. Notion is a generated view, not the working source.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Review delivery health and milestones
- Flag risks, resourcing gaps or budget concerns
- Align on mitigation plans and decisions needed

deliverables.title: Timeline
deliverables.description: Visual representation of key phases, milestones, and dependencies across the engagement.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Timeline structure is generated from Git milestone definitions and sprint configurations (Layer 1). `sprint-status.yaml` is committed to Git by `/bmad-sprint-planning` and tracks epic/story progress. On build engagements, GitHub Actions (Tier 2 Observability) auto-updates milestone progress from Git activity. Static PDF is a Layer 3 generated output from structured milestone data — not manually maintained.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Document scope, deliverables and pricing
- Align on assumptions and dependencies
- Support review and signature process

deliverables.title: Status Report
deliverables.description: Provide ongoing visibility into progress, risks, and upcoming work.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Status reports are auto-generated via GitHub Actions (Tier 2 Observability) triggered by sprint milestones, PR merge events, and weekly schedule. Agent reads Git activity — story progress, PR throughput, open blockers, hours burned — and produces a structured markdown report committed to the repo and posted to Slack. Human PM reviews before external distribution. Smartsheet/PDF are Layer 3 generated formats from the Git source.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Summarize progress and milestones
- Flag risks, blockers and decisions needed
- Outline next steps
- Report hrs burned (if T&M project)

humanGate.action: Reviews auto-generated status report for accuracy before external distribution

deliverables.title: QBR
deliverables.description: Provide ongoing visibility into progress, risks, and upcoming work.
deliverables.aiGeneration: QBR deck is agent-generated from accumulated Git delivery metrics (DORA indicators, sprint velocity, milestone progress), financial actuals, and roadmap progress. Agent synthesizes narrative and produces first-draft slide content. Human PM and Client Partner review and refine. Slides are a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Review progress against goals and KPIs
- Highlight wins, risks and learnings
- Align on upcoming priorities and opportunities

deliverables.title: Completion Memo
deliverables.description: Formal summary of delivered work, outcomes, and closure of the engagement.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Completion Memo is agent-generated from `scope.md`, delivered story records, and committed Git artifacts (Layer 1). Agent harness produces first-draft summary of scope delivered vs. contracted. Human PM reviews and approves. Google Doc is a Layer 3 generated view; Git is the source of record.
deliverables.keyActivities: Summarize scope delivered within SOW and any additional deliverables.

deliverables.title: Opportunity Identification
deliverables.description: A high-level articulation of the business problem or opportunity, grounded in client context, market signals, and competitive dynamics. In ACx engagements, this is read through the lens of the seven ACx Readiness dimensions — Strategy Definition, Data Foundation, Delivery Acceleration, Workforce & AI Proficiency, Industry Depth, Governance & Responsible AI, and Measurement Rigor — to identify where the opportunity is widest and where investment will compound fastest.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-domain-research`, `/bmad-market-research`, and `/bmad-agent-analyst` harness reads raw interview transcripts, workshop notes, and audit inputs → produces structured `findings.md` committed to Git (Layer 1). Discovery readout slide deck is a Layer 3 generated output from structured findings. Human PM gate: review and approve `findings.md` before committing; Delivery Lead reviews deck before client delivery. Miro and NotebookLM remain valid working tools for synthesis sessions.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Immersion - review and analyze:**
- Existing collateral (research, strategy, guidelines, tech debt) to understand the client's market, business, and customer context.
- External market signals (annual statements, competitor press releases, industry reports).
- Conduct experience/tech/data audits to identify current-state limitations.
**Research - plan, execute and analyze:**
- Stakeholder interviews with internal leadership (and external partners) to uncover conflicting priorities, unstated goals, and cultural barriers
- Competitive and comparative research to benchmark against the market and adjacent innovators.
** Synthesize - triangulate findings to:**
- Identify gaps, generate insights, and map future opportunities in the context of broader business objectives and the specific engagement.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck flow:**
- **Title page & Agenda**
- **The journey so far:** quantified summary of effort (e.g. # of weeks, interviews, documents reviewed)
- **Findings:** detailed current-state analysis with "So What" implications for each:
  - Strategy: alignment with business goals and broader initiatives
  - Experience Audit: friction points and gaps in UX, UI, Content.
  - Brand Audit: visual/tonal consistency and market perception.
  - Technology: technical debt, scalability, and integration blockers.
  - Data: quality, accessibility, and actionable insight gaps.
- **Opportunities:** Identification of high-value areas to explore, dig deeper, and refine in the next phase.
- **Next Steps:** actions and timeline

humanGate.action: Review and approve findings.md before committing; review deck before client delivery
humanGate.trigger: Before committing findings.md and before client delivery

deliverables.title: Digital Growth Strategy
deliverables.description: A North Star reference aligning the project with broader org strategy by defining the vision, strategic imperatives, and outcomes. It serves as the primary alignment tool to ensure all subsequent work maps directly back to this core strategy.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-brainstorming` facilitates ideation sessions and commits session outputs to `brainstorming/` in Git (Layer 2 working reference). Research Synthesis Agent consolidates `findings.md`, workshop transcripts, and strategic inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces structured `strategy.md`. Strategy lead reviews and refines via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork). Slide deck — including the placemat visualization — is a Layer 3 generated output from the structured strategy file, not the working document.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Workshops/ working sessions - plan and facilitate:**
- Collaborative sessions with clients to further synthesize immersion findings into the current state and prioritize business drivers.
- Align cross-functional client stakeholders on a core vision and the "non-negotiables" for growth.
 **Strategic placemat creation:**
- Distill complex strategies into a single-page visual framework (The Placemat) defining the vision, strategic imperatives, initiatives, capabilities, outcomes and measures of success.
- Ensure the narrative is punchy, memorable, and easily socialized within the client organization.
 **Future-state capability mapping - identify and define:**
- Organizational, technical, and operational capabilities required to realize the North Star.
- The gap between current-state reality and the future-state requirements.

deliverables.title: Conceptual Design
deliverables.description: An early-stage visual "North Star" that translates abstract goals into a tangible future vision to build client excitement and help secure commitment for build of the project.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Loveable or Figma Make can generate interactive prototypes rapidly from design brief inputs. On engagements with a subsequent build phase, the Design-to-Component pivot applies: Figma/Loveable outputs feed the UX Agent harness via `/bmad-ux` → produces `DESIGN.md` (visual identity: tokens, typography, color) and `EXPERIENCE.md` (interaction patterns, IA, states) committed to Git (Layer 1). `/bmad-spec` distills these into a machine-readable `SPEC.md` for downstream build consumption.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Immersion & Alignment**
- Review the Digital Growth Strategy and Stakeholder Interviews
- Meet with the team to understand what the client is most excited about.
- Align on the direction of the conceptual Design
- Agree on the fidelity of the concept; sketch, wireframe, hi-fi prototype etc.
**Design a Concept**
- Map out the single most important user journey that demonstrates value; we don't design all screens for concepting - just the key ones.
- Complete the concept design in Figma, Loveable etc.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Conceptual Design can live in either:**
1. A slide deck
2. Link to Figma or Loveable
**Conceptual Design can be in the form of:**
1. A low fidelity sketch of a user flow
2. A high fidelity page design
3. An interactive prototype

**The aim is to get the client excited and bought in on the future work.**

deliverables.title: Strategic Initiative Roadmap
deliverables.description: A high level timeline that prioritizes key strategic initiatives across broad time horizons that communicates to an executive audience what is required to achieve a future state for the business.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Roadmap Agent harness reads `strategy.md` and `tech-strategy.md` (Layer 1, Git) → drafts roadmap structure, time horizons, and initiative sequencing for human review. `roadmap.md` committed to Git is the canonical artifact. Human Product and Strategy leads own final prioritization decisions. Google Slides is a Layer 3 generated output from structured roadmap files.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Drafting of Horizon and outcomes**
- Analysis of digital growth strategy document  and technical strategy document, and all relevant strategy inputs.
- Definition of time horizons and associated key objectives over time
**Organize and sequence initiatives and themes**
- Organization of comprehensive themes and initiatives that will deliver on strategy
- Workshopping and prioritization of key initiatives and related dependencies
**Plot and map initiatives and themes over time**
- Roadmap framework development and workstream definition
- Mapping and sequencing of key initiatives that will drive growth strategy
- Team reviews and alignment to roadmap plan
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Executive Summary**
- Description of future state
- Time and horizon definition
- Description of target outcomes by horizon
- Work stream definition that span across horizons
- Roadmap summary outlining key initiatives in each workstream across horizons
- Elaborated details on roadmap items to help illustrate and support (as needed)

deliverables.title: Technical Strategy Document
deliverables.description: Translation of Program or Digital Strategy into technology capabilities needed to deliver the solution.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Tech Synthesis Agent harness reads audit materials, stakeholder interview transcripts, and existing technical documentation → produces `tech-strategy.md` (Layer 1, Git). This structured file is the machine-readable input consumed by the Requirements Agent in the Design & Requirements phase — it must be committed as structured markdown, not only as slides. Slide deck is a Layer 3 generated output. Human Tech Lead gate before client delivery.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Current State Analysis Workshops and Ingestion**
- Current state technical platform ecosystem
- Current state operating / governance model
- Current state capabilities and services
**Platform and Future State Recommendations**
- Evaluate, recommend and integrate platform recommendations
- Address capability gaps in future state capability model
- Define high level future state technical platform ecosystem
**Synthesize- triangulate findings to:**
- Summarize findings, gaps and future state architecture
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Document outline**
- **Executive Summary**
  - Goals and Strategic Objectives
  - Constraints
  - Future State Technical Ecosystem
  - Phasing, roadmap and horizon considerations
- **Current State Findings:**
  - Current state capabilities & Identified gaps
- **Future State**
  - Future state capabilities & ecosystem diagram
  - Platform recommendations and capability mapping
  - Platform vendor evaluation
  - Content management strategy
  - API and integration architecture strategy
  - SEO audit and findings
  - Data, analytics, and insights enablement
  - Changes to operating model
  - Mapping strategy to roadmap / horizons
  - Other considerations

deliverables.title: Primary Research Findings
deliverables.description: A comprehensive report of original data collected from target users and/or stakeholders. This report distills raw evidence into foundational insights that identify unmet needs and market gaps, providing the evidentiary basis to define and/or validate future strategic recommendations.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Research Agent harness supports end-to-end: generates interview guides and screeners from defined research objectives; reads and synthesizes transcripts → produces structured `findings.md` (Layer 1, Git); generates first-draft findings slide deck (Layer 3). `interviews/` folder in Git is the structured artifact consumed by downstream agent harnesses. Human researcher reviews findings for accuracy and insight validity before committing.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Research planning:**
- Define research objectives, participant criteria, and recruitment screener.
- Develop structured interview guides, questionnaires, and fieldwork protocols to ensure unbiased data collection.
- Where necessary assess or onboard external research tools,

**Fieldwork & data collection:**
- Recruit participants
- Execute research through planned qualitative or quantitative methods.

**Analysis & report creation:**
- Triangulate data to generate actionable insights, linking specific user needs or digital capabilities to core business KPIs and strategic outcomes.
- Distill findings into a clear narrative that validates or pivots the current project direction.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- Title page & Agenda
- The journey so far: we are here on the timeline.
**Methodology: summary of what we did (e.g., # of participants, demographics, methodology used).**
- Exec summary of insights: The 3–5 themes or key findings discovered during the research.
- Detailed Findings: Deep dives into finding themes, supported by:
**Direct quotes/evidence: voice of the customer.**
- The so what implications
- Recommendations: actionable next steps based on validated findings.
- Next Steps: actions and timeline

humanGate.action: Reviews findings for accuracy and insight validity

deliverables.title: Content/Brand Strategy
deliverables.description: A deep dive into the definition of the brand voice, messaging pillars, and how content serves user needs.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Content Strategy Agent harness reads existing brand materials, audit outputs, and persona definitions from Git → produces structured content pillars, messaging framework, and content audit analysis. Human Content Strategist reviews and refines — expert editorial judgment is required to complete. Agent output is a substantive first draft, not a finished deliverable.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Content Audit (in excel)**
- Inventory & Mapping: Document all existing pages and categorize them by type (e.g., educational, transactional, support).
- Keep/Kill/Combine Logic: Evaluate each page against current user needs and business goals to determine its future state.
- Gap Analysis: Identify what content is missing that is required to fulfill

**Brand Material Audit**
- Consistency Check: Review existing brand materials to identify discrepancies in voice, tone, and visual messaging.
- Pillar Extraction: Isolate recurring themes or successful messaging patterns that should be carried forward.

**Strategy Development**
- Persona Alignment: Map specific messaging pillars to the unique pain points of each user persona.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- **Title page & Agenda**
- **The journey so far:** Outline process; what goes into this deliverable?
- **Overview of Personas or Audiences**
- **Content Audit Overview**
  - Show summary of total pages
  - Keep/Kill/Combine Overview
  - Gaps analysis: what is working well and what needs improvement
- **Proposed Content/Brand Strategy**
  - Breakdown of Principles or Themes paired with recommendations
- **Page Level or Component Level Sketches**
  - Show examples of proposed layouts (before and after)
- **Next Steps**

deliverables.title: Rebrand/Stylescapes
deliverables.description: High-level "visual landscapes" in the form of a 1 page style sheet or a homepage that combine colors, typography, and imagery to show a brand's direction.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-brainstorming` facilitates creative ideation sessions and commits session outputs to `brainstorming/` in Git (Layer 2 working reference). Midjourney, VEO, and Flora remain valid for video/animation asset generation. Designer leads creative direction; agent accelerates reference curation and initial concept generation. Human Designer owns final creative decisions.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Strategic Translation:**
- Review the Content/Brand Strategy (if any) to ensure visual elements match the defined brand voice and messaging pillars.
- Review any existing brand guidelines or Design Systems in place.
  **Moodboarding:**
- Curate a broad collection of references (photography style, UI patterns, color palettes) to pressure-test different "vibes" before committing to a full stylescape.
  **Stylescape Creation:**
- Create two visual concepts using a blank canvas or the home page.
- Share with client to gain feedback and iterate.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma File**
- Title page
- What are we going through today?
- How do stylescapes work? What are they? How do you do them?
- Caveats:
  - "This is just a stylescape, copy and content is FPO".
  - This will have X many rounds of reviews.
- Version 1 and 2
  - 2 version of Stylescapes
- Next Steps

deliverables.title: Ecosystem Map
deliverables.description: A visualization of all digital and physical touchpoints within the customer's assembled experience ecosystem. In ACx terms, brands do not own the customer's full context — they occupy a node in an ecosystem the customer assembled: across multiple brands, platforms, relationships, and moments simultaneously. This map names every node, identifies which the brand owns versus influences, and locates where agentic intelligence can earn a role the customer cannot easily reassemble elsewhere.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-brainstorming` facilitates discovery ideation and commits session outputs to `brainstorming/` in Git (Layer 2 working reference). Agent harness reads stakeholder interview transcripts, technical documentation, and data flow inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces draft ecosystem inventory mapping owned, shared, and transformational brand touchpoints — including touchpoints the brand does not control but the customer depends on. Figma Make or Lovable can be used for interactive visualization. Human Strategy and Tech leads review for completeness and accuracy before client delivery.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- **Stakeholder Workshops:**
  - Conduct workshops or interviews with key stakeholders to identify all known customer touchpoints.
  - Review internal documentation to uncover hidden data silos or secondary digital platforms.
- **Current State Inventory:**
  - Catalog all digital properties (apps, sites, portals) and physical interactions (in-store, support calls, mailers).
  - Identify the primary actors (users, employees, third-party vendors) involved at each stage.
- **Data Flow & Integration Mapping:**
  - Trace how information moves between systems (e.g., CRM to Email Marketing tool).
- **Synthesis & Visualization:**
  - Draft the map in Figma or MIRO to create a cohesive "universe" of the user experience.
  - Apply visual hierarchy to distinguish between primary, secondary, and background touchpoints.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Link to Miro or Figma. Link should contain:**
- **Intro to Map**
  - Purpose
  - Intent
- **Static or Interactive Map**
  - Outlining the main properties of the digital experience

deliverables.title: Journey Map
deliverables.description: A map of the customer's experience across all relevant touchpoints and moments, revealing the emotional and functional arc of their relationship with the brand. Linear funnel thinking is a limiting model in the ACx era — customers have ongoing, parallel, context-dependent relationships with brands, not a path through them. This deliverable maps presence across the full ecosystem the customer has assembled, identifying where continuity breaks, where context resets, and where agentic intelligence can earn a role that compounds with every interaction.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Journey Map Agent harness reads `findings.md` and persona definitions (Layer 1, Git) → produces draft journey stages, user actions, thinking/feeling layers, and friction point identification. Human Design and Strategy leads validate against user research evidence and refine the emotional journey. Agent output is a structured first draft; human expertise is required for the empathy and insight layer.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Note: usually paired with User Research unless the research has already been conducted by the client and passed to us.**

**Identifying User "Stages":**
- Phase Definition: Establish high-level phases (e.g. Awareness, Discovery, Purchase, Retention, Advocacy).
- Goal Alignment: Define user objectives for each phase.

**Map "Doing, Thinking, Feeling" Layers:**
- User Actions: Detail the specific steps and touchpoints the user interacts with (e.g. visiting site, or receiving an email).
- Mindset: Document the user's thoughts and questions at each stage (e.g., "I need information I can use right now").
- Emotional Journey: Capture emotional states to identify delight or high-stress moments.

**Identifying Friction Points and Opportunities:**
- Pain Point Discovery: Spot where users get stuck or experience conversion declines (e.g., cart abandonment).
- Opportunity Synthesis: Translate friction into actionable "How Might We" statements to improve future experience.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**PDF should include the following:**
- **Context & Methodology**
- **User Persona:**  Profile summary (name, goals, expectations) the map represents.
- **The Scenario: **Definition of the specific journey explored (e.g. "First-time purchase flow").
- **The Visual Journey Map (The Core Output)**
- **Horizontal Axis:** chronological Journey Stages.
- **Vertical Lanes:**
  - Actions: Step-by-step behaviors.
  - Touchpoints: Channels used (Web, Mobile, App).
  - The "Experience" Curve: Visual graph of user sentiment (Highs and Lows).
  - Thinking/Feeling Layers: Quotes and internal monologues from the user.
    **Key Findings & Strategic Recommendations**
- Moments that Matter:Identification of critical "make or break" moments (emotional & functional)
- Prioritized Friction Points: A list of blockers tied to real business impact.
- Roadmap of Opportunities: Strategic features or process changes for Downstream Deliverables (e.g., Product Brief or Story Map).

deliverables.title: Service Blueprint
deliverables.description: A visual map detailing the end-to-end service journey, connecting user touchpoints to the specific behind-the-scenes processes and systems required to deliver them. It aligns cross-functional teams on how the front-end experience is operationally supported.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Service Blueprint Agent harness reads `findings.md`, `strategy.md`, and technical architecture inputs (Layer 1, Git) → drafts front-stage journey stages, user actions, outcomes, and initial backstage process mapping. Human cross-functional team validates backstage accuracy in workshop. Agent-produced draft significantly reduces facilitation prep time.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Blueprint template**
- Front-end layers: Future-state journey, User need state/mindset, Key actions & interaction
  - Optional: content, comms and other channels/touchpoints, key outcomes
- Backstage layers: Systems & infrastructure, Data orchestration
  - Optional:  Operations & People, KPIs
**Slide deck**
- Title page & Agenda
- The journey so far: we are here on the timeline.
- Navigating the blueprint: how to read the artefact, guide of the various layers and why it is valuable.
- Link to full blueprint miro
- Optional: translate full blueprint into slide deck, capturing all the vertical  information per journey stage onto one slide.
- Next Steps: actions and timeline

deliverables.title: Value Case
deliverables.description: A structured business case for the proposed initiative — quantifying expected return, risk, and strategic fit. In ACx engagements, the Value Case is anchored to the three ACx success criteria: Strategy (AI capability connected to a named customer outcome with a baseline), Delivery Execution (time from brief to working system), and Industry Depth (vertical knowledge that makes outputs commercially meaningful). The output establishes a 90-Day ROI Validation commitment — measurable lift attributable within the first quarter of deployment — and maps expected Velocity Multiple gains as the agentic capability matures.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-brainstorming` supports assumption discovery sessions committed to `brainstorming/` in Git (Layer 2 working reference). Financial modelling agent harness reads `strategy.md`, `tech-strategy.md`, and SOW scope inputs → generates baseline assumptions model and sensitivity analysis structure. Agent runs best/expected/worst-case scenario simulations from defined variables. Human Strategy and Data leads validate assumptions and review model logic before client presentation. Excel model is the working artifact; slides are a Layer 3 generated output.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Baseline & assumption- gather & document:**
Historical performance data, current operational costs, and growth projections.
- Foundational assumptions (e.g., average order value, customer acquisition cost, internal labor rates)

**Financial modelling - define & calculate:**
Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) incl build, licensing, maintenance, and internal operational overhead.
- Calculate projected revenue uplift and cost savings by applying assumptions and  industry benchmarks
- Risk-adjust the projected returns by modelling Best, Expected, and Worst-case scenarios (Sensitivity Analysis).

**Model validation:**
- Conduct validation sessions with client stakeholders to pressure-test assumptions and ensure the logic aligns with their internal reporting standards.

deliverables.title: Executive Summary
deliverables.description: A summary of the whole project's core findings, strategic recommendations, and projected impact. It provides our clients the ability to share back with their executive stakeholders to communicate value and drive alignment and action.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Synthesis Agent harness reads all committed deliverable files in Git (Layer 1) — `findings.md`, `strategy.md`, `tech-strategy.md`, `product-brief.md`, `roadmap.md` — and produces a structured first-draft executive summary. Human Strategy lead reviews, refines narrative, and approves before client delivery. Slide deck is a Layer 3 generated output. NotebookLM remains a valid working tool for synthesis sessions prior to committing structured files.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Review & synthesize:**
- Review all project deliverables, strategic recommendations, and key decisions made throughout the engagement.
- Extract the most critical proof points and insights that support the final strategic direction.
**Narrative & summary development:**
- Summarize all workstreams into a concise, impactful narrative.
- Distill complex technical and experience findings into high-level business outcomes.
- Structure the summary to move quickly from the problem to the solution and the Value, ensuring it is optimized for high-level stakeholder socialization.

deliverables.title: Change Management Strategy
deliverables.description: A structured plan for introducing, driving adoption of, and sustaining organizational and process change, aligning people, communications, and training to support successful transformation outcomes.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Change Strategy Agent harness reads `brief.md`, `findings.md`, and strategic roadmap inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces structured stakeholder impact analysis, readiness assessment, and communications plan framework. `change-strategy.md` committed to Git is the canonical artifact consumed by the Change Management Enablement harness (409). Human Strategy lead reviews and approves before client delivery.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Define change vision, success metrics, and adoption milestones articulate and align**
- Translate objectives into a clear change narrative that explains the "why," the future state, and what will be different for teams day-to-day.
- Establish measurable success criteria, behavioral indicators, and adoption milestones to track progress beyond go-live.
**Map stakeholder impact, readiness, and resistance risks - assess and prioritize**
- Identify impacted audiences, roles, and workflows to understand the scale and nature of change across the org..
- Evaluate readiness, and potential resistance to inform tailored engagement and mitigation strategies
**Develop communications, training, and reinforcement plans - design and activate**
- Create a comms  by audience, channel, and moment in the journey.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- **Title & agenda:** Frame objectives, scope, and decisions required from stakeholders.
- **Journey so far: **Visual timeline showing discovery, strategy, design, and activation with "you are here" orientation.
- **Change context:** Summary of business drivers, impacted groups, and critical dependencies shaping the approach.
- **Change vision & outcomes:** Articulation of the future state, success metrics, and adoption milestones.
- **Stakeholder landscape:** High-level view of audiences, impact levels, and engagement approach.
- **Governance & next steps:** define change ambassadors and accountability for sustaining the change.

deliverables.title: ACx Readiness Assessment
deliverables.description: A scored readiness assessment across the seven ACx Architecture Map dimensions — Strategy Definition, Data Foundation, Delivery Acceleration, Workforce & AI Proficiency, Industry Depth, Governance & Responsible AI, and Measurement Rigor — establishing a baseline and prioritized gap list before ACx investment is sequenced. Average enterprise scores 2.9/10; ACx Leaders score 7+. The gap between those numbers defines the engagement.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent reads stakeholder inputs, existing documentation, and technology landscape (Layer 1, Git) → scores all seven ACx Readiness dimensions (1–10), identifies gaps against ACx Leader benchmarks (7+/10 average), and produces `acx-readiness.md` with a prioritized investment sequencing recommendation mapped to H1 (Prove), H2 (Scale), and H3 (Transform) horizons. `/bmad-market-research` pulls external reference data; `/bmad-product-brief` structures findings into the briefing format. PM and Agentic Champion gate: validate scores against internal knowledge before presenting to client.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Dimension Scoring (1–10 across 7 dimensions)**
- Strategy Definition: Is AI investment tied to a named customer outcome with a baseline and accountable owner?
- Data Foundation: Is first-party data unified with agentic access layers?
- Delivery Acceleration: Can the org move from brief to working system in 8 weeks?
- Workforce & AI Proficiency: Are 100% of practitioners trained, badged, and role-credentialed?
- Industry Depth: Is vertical expertise embedded in delivery teams, not just strategy decks?
- Governance & Responsible AI: Is AI governance an enabler, not a compliance exercise?
- Measurement Rigor: Are AI outcomes measured — not just AI activity?
**Gap Analysis & Horizon Sequencing**
- Identify dimensions below 5 (critical gaps) and 5–7 (scale gaps)
- Map remediation investments to H1/H2/H3 horizons
- Produce recommended portfolio posture for client review
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**`acx-readiness.md` including:**
- Executive dimension scores (radar chart format in presentation layer)
- Gap vs ACx Leader benchmark per dimension
- Top 3 priority investments by horizon
- Recommended H1 quick win (must be achievable within one quarter)

humanGate.action: Validate dimension scores against client context; prioritize gaps for horizon sequencing
humanGate.trigger: Before ACx portfolio planning and engagement scoping

deliverables.title: Product Brief
deliverables.description: A foundational document that defines the product's objectives, target audience, and success metrics. It ensures all cross-functional teams are aligned on the "why" and "what" before significant resources are committed to the "how."
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-product-brief` reads `findings.md`, `strategy.md`, `tech-strategy.md`, and interview transcripts (Layer 1, Git) → produces structured `brief.md` with `addendum.md` (personas, rejected alternatives, sizing) and `.decision-log.md` (append-only decision audit trail). Human PM and Strategy lead review and approve via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork) before proceeding to story mapping. Google Doc is a Layer 3 generated view for client-facing review — not the working document.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Stakeholder engagement and information gathering**
- Facilitate workshops to align on user needs and solution level goals and objectives
- Success mapping, defining KPIs , and prioritization criteria
**Epic Generation**
- Define the outcomes (business and user needs) the solution is seeking to deliver.
- Describe the scope associated epics that connect to business goals and prioritized sequence of epics that will deliver value
**Constraints and dependencies definition**
- Describe the capability and/or technical constraints that exist for the solution that need to be accommodated for
- List all current assumptions and open questions that exist with the solution.
**Design Brief**
- Outline design guidance and template and component strategy
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- Title page & Table of Contents
- Business Context and Product vision
- Key Objectives and KPIs
- Description of User types and needs
- List of prioritization criteria
- Epic level descriptions that include core user goals and value drivers
- List of current assumptions and open questions to be addressed
- Design team scope and guiding principles

humanGate.action: Review and approve brief.md via Cowork

deliverables.title: Information Architecture
deliverables.description: The structural map of navigation across a website.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent harness reads `brief.md` and content audit inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces draft site structure and IA hierarchy for designer review. Agent accelerates initial structure generation from defined user types, content inventory, and navigation patterns. Human Designer leads final IA decisions and client validation sessions.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Outline the current state IA in Figma.**
**Create the future state IA:**
- **Organization Systems:** Choose a structural format, such as Hierarchical (broad to specific), Sequential (step-by-step), or Matrix (multiple user choices).
- **Sitemap Construction:** Create a visual graphic showing the relationship between "parent" and "child" pages across the entire site.
**  Validation and Testing: (optional)**
- **Card Sorting:** Conduct exercises where users organize content into their own sensible groupings to reveal their "mental models".
- **Tree Testing:** Have users attempt to find specific information using only the link names to validate if category names are easy to understand.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma Presentation**
- Title page & Agenda
- What is an IA? How do we read one?
- Current State IA
- What went into building the future state?
  - Outline gaps from Experience Audit
  - Outline points from the Stakeholder Interviews
  - Outline key points from the Data (if any)
- Future State IA
- Next Steps

deliverables.title: Requirements
deliverables.description: A high level outline of the functional requirements that prioritized for project planning purposes. It serves as the source of truth to align the team on scope, identify MVP boundaries, and ensure no functional gaps exist in the proposed solution.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/apply-requirements-system` reads `brief.md` and stakeholder inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces `requirements.json`, a structured machine-readable requirements register organized by epic, user type, and priority. Agent output populates Storiesonboard for human review and prioritization. `requirements.json` in Git is the canonical artifact consumed downstream by `/bmad-create-epics-and-stories` and sprint planning. `/bmad-spec` distills the requirements into a machine-readable `SPEC.md` capability contract.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Cross Functional Workshops**
- Facilitate cross-functional reviews with stakeholders to review breadth and depth of functionality based on epics from the product brief.
- Facilitate workshops to review and prioritize high-level functional scope across releases
- Define key user flows and states that outlines how the solution  should function
**Story mapping**
- Drafting of high level user stories across all user types and epics outlined in the product brief
- Individual user stories describe the functionality by completing "I want to…"
- Prioritization criteria organization user stories from highest to lowest priority so scope lines can be drawn.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Storiesonboard spreadsheet including:**
- Users
- Epics
- User Activities
- High Level User Stories
- Prioritization and release lines

humanGate.action: Review and prioritize user stories; define release scope lines

deliverables.title: Technical Architecture Document
deliverables.description: Detailed application, data, security, infrastructure and operations architecture documented for the Solution.
deliverables.aiGeneration:
`/bmad-create-architecture` reads `brief.md`, `prd.md`, and design inputs (Layer 1, Git) → produces `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md`, the architectural contract defining paradigm, technology invariants, and Architecture Decision records. `/bmad-spec` distills the architecture into a machine-readable `SPEC.md` capability kernel consumed by dev agents. 

**Designed for Agents (ACx +02):** The TAD must explicitly address agent-readiness as a first-class architectural concern — machine-readable content structures, semantic APIs with deterministic pricing and policy contracts, structured product and brand data parseable by third-party agents, and identity resolution infrastructure that supports agent-to-agent protocols. These are Phase 01 foundation decisions (ACx 2026 horizon) that determine what is possible in Mode 02 (agent-led) interactions from 2027 onward. Human Tech Lead and PM gate: architecture review before implementation begins.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Requirements Mapping**
- Review Product Brief, User Story Map, Technical Strategy
**Workshops to map user stories to APIs, integration workflows, data models, sequence diagrams and classes**
- Workshops to identify APIs to either be consumed or developed
**Evolving the TAD through Build:**
- TAD should be updated throughout the build process to reflect any architecture changes
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Document Outline**
- **Executive Summary**
  - High Level Architecture: System Diagram & Integration Map
  - Infrastructure: Cloud Provider, Architecture Overview, Infrastructure As Code, Security and Permissions, Environments
- **Backend Services:** Architecture and Patterns, Backend language and Framework, Data Persistence, Migrations, Authentication, API Specifications, Unit Tests, Webhooks and Asynchronous Services, Event Driven Services, Email Service, Notification Services, Admin Tools
- **Frontend Application:** Application framework, State and Data Management, Rendering Strategy, Localization and Internationalization, SEO, Styling, Responsive Design, Assets Optimization, Accessibility, Unit Tests, Code Standards, Design System, Atomic Design Principles, Variant Authority, Themes and Customizations, Icons and Fonts, Versioning
- **Data and Analytics:** Consent Management Platform, Analytics Solution. Data Acquisition, Injection, Storage, Transformation Service, Data Catalog and Observability, Reporting & Business Intelligence Platform, AI/ML/LLM Service, Testing & Personalization Platform, Consumer Data Platform (CDP), Marketing Automation Platform (MA), Data Sharing Service
- **Third Party Integrations:** CMS, DAM, Search, Video Streaming, Forms and Lead Capturing, Social Media Integrations, Cookie and Privacy Compliance (Consent Management)
- **Delivery Methodology and Tools:** Code Repository, Branching Strategy, Code Review Process, CI/CD Pipeline, Feature Flags, Static Code Analysis, Quality Engineering

deliverables.title: Production Design
deliverables.description: Pixel-perfect screen designs ready for developer handoff, including all states.
deliverables.aiGeneration: **Design-to-Component Pivot (build projects only):** On engagements with a build phase, Figma is the exploration and iteration tool but the designer's final deliverable is functional UI component code viewable in Storybook — not Figma files. `/bmad-ux` reads Figma via MCP or design notes → produces `DESIGN.md` (visual design system: colors, typography, spacing, component anatomy) and `EXPERIENCE.md` (interaction design: IA, behavior, states, accessibility, journeys) committed to Git (Layer 1). `/bmad-spec` distills these into `SPEC.md` — the machine-readable interaction contract. Components are built and committed to `src/components/`. Storybook is the Layer 3 generated view for stakeholder review. Tech Lead and Designer jointly approve component output before Developer Agent stories reference it. For design-only projects, Figma remains the deliverable as specified.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**High-Fidelity UI Creation:**
- Translate low-fidelity Wireframes (309) into high-fidelity "North Star" screens.
- Review Page designs
- Build out all necessary functional states, including hover effects, active buttons, empty states, and error handling.
**Responsive Adaptation:**
- Design layouts for different breakpoints (Mobile, Tablet, Desktop) to ensure the Conceptual Design (203) translates across all devices. (This is SOW dependant)
**Handoff Preparation:**
- Clean up layers and naming conventions for the Component Library creation.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma File**
- Figma File should be broken down in the way the Product Brief outlines it.
- All Pages are broken down into hi-fidelity screens for desktop and mobile at the minimum.

deliverables.title: Component Library
deliverables.description: A collection of reusable UI elements (buttons, inputs, etc.) within a Figma, accompanied by a Storybook.
deliverables.aiGeneration: **Applies when Design-to-Component pivot is active.** Git (`src/components/`) is the canonical source (Layer 1). Storybook is the Layer 3 generated view replacing the traditional Figma-only handoff. Component scaffolding is agent-assisted — the Design-to-Component onboarding skill bootstraps Storybook environment, component structure, and initial token setup. Figma file remains a Layer 2 working artifact for the design team.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Design to create Figma file; break page designs own into atoms, molecules and organisms & annotate where needed.
- Development should prepare a Storybook that pairs with the Component Library.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma File should be broken down in the way the Product Brief outlines it.**
**Figma File Should Contain:**
- Design Token System
- Library of Components broken down atomically
- Page Designs (broken into Atoms, Molecules + Organisms Templates)
- Desktop and Mobile Breakpoints outlined + Tablet Breakpoint
- Figma Component Documentation (Annotations, Do's & Don'ts)
- Figma Governance (Min)
- Storybook - provided by tech team

deliverables.title: Measurement Plan & Analytics Tracking Design
deliverables.description: Defines what to measure, why it matters, and how it is tracked across digital products to ensure reliable analytics, attribution, and performance measurement.
deliverables.aiGeneration:
Agent harness reads `prd.md`, `brief.md`, and stakeholder KPI inputs (Layer 1, Git) → drafts the measurement architecture: event taxonomy, data layer specification, and KPI definitions. 

**ACx Metrics:** For ACx engagements, this deliverable establishes the six new measurement constructs that replace legacy CX metrics — (1) **90-Day ROI Validation** (revenue lift or cost reduction measurable and attributable within 90 days, replacing annual retrospectives); (2) **Experience Continuity Score** (whether the system carries customer context across sessions, channels, and time, replacing per-touchpoint NPS); (3) **Time to Value** (kickoff to working system in production, replacing time-to-recommendation); (4) **Velocity Multiple** (AI-augmented output volume ÷ human baseline at equivalent quality, replacing headcount growth); (5) **AI Contribution Rate** (% of customer-facing output generated or materially enhanced by AI, replacing AI adoption rate); (6) **AI Delivery Rate** (complex interactions resolved by agentic AI without escalation, replacing cost-per-contact). Human PM and Analytics lead gate: KPI sign-off and baseline establishment before deployment begins.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**KPI & Outcome Alignment Section**
- Define north star and supporting KPIs
- Map objectives to leading & lagging indicators
- Establish success criteria per use case
- Define ROI and attribution logic upfront

**Design the Event & Analytics Architecture**
- Develop enterprise event taxonomy & naming standards & Tracking Matrix
- Define required properties & data layer specifications
- Map cross-channel tracking (web, app, backend, POS)
- Define compliance & attribution logic

**Governance & QA**
- Define tracking implementation approach
- Translate event taxonomy into engineering-ready specifications
- Define tracking validation framework
- Identify implementation risks & dependencies
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Google Sheet**
- Outcome & OKR Layer
- Measurement Strategy
- Tracking Matrix and Event Design
- Developer Implementation Readiness
- Platform Configuration

deliverables.title: Product Feature Roadmap
deliverables.description: A prioritized timeline of product capabilities and enhancements aligned with business goals. It serves as a strategic communication tool that bridges the gap between high-level vision and tactical execution.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Roadmap Agent harness reads `epics.md`, `brief.md`, and epic priorities (Layer 1, Git) → generates RICE scoring inputs and initial roadmap sequencing for team review. `roadmap.md` committed to Git (Layer 1) is the canonical artifact. Human Product lead owns final prioritization decisions. Slide deck is a Layer 3 generated output from structured roadmap files.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Roadmap drafting and cross-functional alignment
- Sequencing and mapping of epic level scope over time to plan out sprint priorities
- Organization of epic level scope into swim lanes of work streams
- Dependency mapping for key technical or data integrations required for roadmap success
- Team reviews and alignment to plan
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- Epic level scope sequenced and mapped over sprint time intervals
- Epic level scope organized in workstreams
- Dependencies and milestones plotted for delivery team tracking

deliverables.title: Wireframes
deliverables.description: Low-fidelity layouts that define structure, hierarchy, and content placement. Wireframes must be at the fidelity where all field level information should be outlined to support the content modeller.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Wireframe generation agent (Figma Make, Loveable, or equivalent) produces initial low-fidelity layouts from `brief.md` and IA structure. Designer reviews and iterates. On build projects, `/bmad-ux` extracts interaction contracts from wireframe annotations → produces `EXPERIENCE.md` (component behavior, states, IA) committed to Git (Layer 1). `/bmad-spec` derives `SPEC.md` stubs per component area. Wireframe component annotations map to the interaction catalog in `EXPERIENCE.md` to ensure alignment with the Design-to-Component pipeline.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Create low-mid fidelity mockups of pages.
- Participate in client reviews to finalize the information hierarchy and functionality of key pages.
- Create Figma file to house the wireframes.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma File should be broken down in the way the Product Brief outlines it.**
- **Figma File:**
  - Wireframes must be attached to a component list that is IDed to each component being built.
    - Components should point to either SHADCN or Cafe.
  - Wireframes must be annotated; purpose, questions etc. must be outlined.
  - Wireframes must be at the fidelity where all field level information should be outlined to support the content modeller.

deliverables.title: Content Copywriting
deliverables.description: Copy and content for the solution aligned to content strategy and brand voice. Optional: SEO tags.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Copywriting Agent harness takes an AI-first approach: reads persona definitions, content strategy, and page hierarchy from Git → produces first-draft copy per page. Copy is committed to Git or CMS as structured content. Human copywriter reviews for brand voice, accuracy, and SEO optimization. Copy tracker and review cycles are structured around Git-backed content states.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Write copy for pages of the product.
- Organize all the client revisions for copy via a copy tracker.
- Participate in client reviews.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Copy Tracker Should Outline:**
- Page Inventory: A list of all unique indexable pages, organized by locale (e.g., US, CA, BR).
- Link to Copy Deck per page
- URL Mapping: Current URL vs. New Redirect URL.
- Authoring Status: A workflow tracker for "Not Started," "In Progress," "Ready for Review," and "Published".
**Copy Decks Should Contain:**
- The page purpose
- Intended audience
- Page copy broken into hierarchy: H1, H2 etc.
- ALT Text for images.
- Component annotation; which component is each content block using.
- Optional (SEO tags)

deliverables.title: User Testing
deliverables.description: Usability testing of designs and prototypes to validate with users before build.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Research Agent harness generates discussion guides, screeners, and test plans from `brief.md` and user type definitions (Layer 1, Git). Agent synthesizes test session transcripts into structured findings post-research. Human researcher leads facilitation and validates insight interpretation. Agent output reduces planning overhead significantly.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Develop a discussion guide and screener
- Conduct usability testing with users; could be IA Testing, A/B Testing, Copy Testing, Concept Testing etc.
- Create a summary of findings, insights and recommendations.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Test Plan:**
- **Screener:** Archetypes, Behavioral Qualification, Technical Requirements, Logistics & Compliance and Articulation.
- **Discussion Guide:**
  - Introduction & Setup: Moderator Briefing & Consent, Logistics & Recording Permissions, Bias Mitigation
  - Core Testing Questions
  - Wrap Up
**Summary of Findings:**
**Section 1: Executive Summary**
- **Vision:** 1-sentence goal of the initiative.
- **The Problem:** The specific gap between current digital tools and guest needs.
**Section 2: Methodology at a Glance**
- Scope: Number of participants and key demographics (e.g., 10 participants: High-Value vs. New Guests).
- Activities: Testing duration and format (e.g., 10 weeks, 75-minute 1:1 sessions).
**Section 3: Concept Overviews (The Prototypes)**
**Section 4: Key Insights**
**Section 5: Synthesis & Outcomes**
**Slide 6: Unmet Needs & Friction**
**Section 7: Roadmap & Strategic Recommendations**

deliverables.title: Design Systems
deliverables.description: Reusable design standards, components, and documentation that ensure consistency and efficiency across the product.
deliverables.aiGeneration: **Applies when Design-to-Component pivot is active.** Design token definitions are committed to Git as `design/tokens/` (Layer 1). Design System Agent harness reads component structure and token definitions → produces documentation framework and component usage guidelines. Storybook (Layer 3 generated) serves as the primary documentation layer on build projects, supplementing or replacing ZeroHeight/Supernova. Figma file remains a Layer 2 working artifact for the design team.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Design to create Figma file; break page designs own into atoms, molecules and organisms. 2+ breakpoints outlined.
- Design to thoroughly document pages and components using documentation tool e.g. Zero Height, Confluence etc.
- Development to prepare a Storybook for the client.
- Review components with clients via client review cycles.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma File should be broken down in the way the Product Brief outlines it.**
**Figma File Should Contain:**
- Design Token System
- Library of Figma Components
- Page Designs (include full componentization Atoms, Molecules + Organisms + Templates.
- Desktop and Mobile Breakpoints outlined + Tablet Breakpoint  + additional breakpoints
- Figma Component Documentation (Robust Documentations) - optionally in ZeroHeight
- Governance Documentation (Optionally in Notion, ZeroHeight/Supernova  etc.)

deliverables.title: UI Kit
deliverables.description: Visual component library and patterns for consistent UI implementation.
deliverables.aiGeneration: **Applies when Design-to-Component pivot is active.** UI Kit components are committed to `src/components/` in Git (Layer 1). Page Style Guide is generated from design tokens and component definitions. Figma file is a Layer 2 working artifact; Storybook is the canonical Layer 3 view for developer reference.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Design to create Figma file; break page designs own into atoms, molecules and organisms
- Create 1 page style guide
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Figma file should contain:**
- Page Style Guide
- Library of Figma Components
- Page Designs (include pages broken into Atoms and Molecules)
**Desktop and Mobile Breakpoints outlined**
- Component States outlined (default, hover, disabled, pressed, focus)

deliverables.title: Product Requirements Document (PRD)
deliverables.description: Single source of truth for functional and non-functional requirements, acceptance criteria, and scope for the solution.

external: Notion page, GitHub Wiki, or interactive SPA (per-project decision)

deliverables.aiGeneration: **Gold standard: BMAD PRD workflow.** `/bmad-prd` reads all structured inputs from Git (Layer 1) — `brief.md`, `findings.md`, `tech-strategy.md`, `epics.md`, `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md`, and measurement plan — and generates a complete, structured `prd.md` to BMAD standards, with `addendum.md` (personas, rejected alternatives) and `.decision-log.md` (append-only decisions). `/bmad-spec` derives `SPEC.md` — the machine-readable 5-field kernel that development agents consume. Human PM and Product lead review and approve via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork). The client-consumable PRD is a Layer 3 generated output (Notion page, GitHub Wiki, or interactive SPA — format is a per-project decision). Client feedback flows back into `prd.md`, not into the generated view.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Run BMAD PRD workflow against Git-structured inputs (`brief.md`, `findings.md`, `epics.md`, `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md`) to generate `prd.md`
- Human PM and Product lead review generated PRD for completeness, accuracy, and scope alignment via the Interface layer (e.g., Cowork)
- Decompose High-level user stories prioritized for release to a level that can be sized for estimations
- Associate key assumptions to user stories so that work efforts can be appropriately sized
- Define all non-functional requirements and platform constraints that exist for the solution to be built
- Publish client-consumable Layer 3 view and collect structured feedback into Git
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Git file: `prd.md`**
- Title page & Table of Contents
- Product Description and Overview
- Product Brief Summary & link
- List of Epic and User Stories that include:
  - User Story Descriptions
  - Associated Assumptions
  - Assigned level of priority and effort
- Product Feature Roadmap Summary & assumptions
- List of Non-Functional Requirements
  - Performance requirements
  - Security and privacy
  - Analytics and Accessibility
- Technical Constraints & Dependencies
  - Platform support and integrations
- Design Requirements
  - Description and assumptions for design needed to build solution
- Launch considerations

humanGate.action: Review and approve prd.md via Cowork

deliverables.title: Implementation Stories
deliverables.description: A singular source of truth for the delivery team to draw detailed user stories (prioritized in the JIRA backlog) into active sprints to execute the sprint plan specified in the product feature roadmap.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Developer Agent harness reads `epics.md`, `prd.md`, and acceptance criteria definitions (Layer 1, Git) → elaborates detailed JIRA-ready user stories with business rules, acceptance criteria in Gherkin format, and design/technical references. Agent output populates JIRA (Layer 3 view) for sprint planning. Human PM and Tech Lead gate: story refinement and pointing before sprint commitment.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Detailed User Story Elaborations**
- Drafting of detailed user stories in JIRA into individual work units that meet definition ready criteria
- Linking and alignment to designs and technical architecture documentation
- Review and briefing of QA leads to inform test plans
**Backlog Grooming and Refinement**
- Team reviews of user stories and refinement of details to ensure clear understanding of work and definition of done
- Story pointing and alignment of work efforts for sprint planning
**Sprint Planning and subTask definition**
- Story pointing and alignment of work efforts for sprint planning
- Sub-task definition associated to user stories as part of sprint kick-off
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**JIRA User Stories that are inclusive of:**
- Linked to Epics
- Detailed description and business rules
- Acceptance Criteria in Gherkin format
- Design and technical reference
- Accessibility requirements
- Link to test cases
- Linked Subtasks
- Storypointed (anything over 8 pts is too big and should be broken down to another user story)

deliverables.title: Solution Implementation
deliverables.description: Working, deployable and potentially launchable "solution" where solution could be a web site, a mobile app, a platform ecosystem, an e-commerce solution, a data warehouse implementation, etc.

internal: Project repo, documentation, unit tests, development environment

deliverables.aiGeneration: Developer Agent harness (Claude Code or equivalent) reads `prd.md`, `SPEC.md`, `epics.md`, and `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md` (Layer 1, Git) → implements features, unit tests, and devops scripts via PR workflow. `sprint-status.yaml` is updated by `/bmad-sprint-status` each sprint cycle. Human engineers review and approve PRs — this is the human gate before merge. `/bmad-tea` runs automated validation on every PR via CI/CD pipeline. Sprint status, PR throughput, and DORA metrics are surfaced via Tier 1–3 Observability; no separate reporting artifact required during active build.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Implementing solution framework based on information architecture, technical architecture, strategic objectives and non-functional requirements
- Implementing features based on provided epics, user stories and tasks
- Remediating issues discovered during development, testing and UAT
- Packaging solution so that it can be deployed
deliverables.structuralFramework: N/A

humanGate.action: Review and approve PRs before merge

deliverables.title: UAT
deliverables.description: User acceptance testing with client to identify defects, content issues, missed requirements or other non-functional requirements.
deliverables.aiGeneration: QA Agent harness generates UAT test scripts and scenario walkthroughs from acceptance criteria in `prd.md` (Layer 1, Git) for the client team to execute. Agent tracks and categorizes defects logged during UAT sessions and produces a structured defect summary and resolution status report. Human client team owns test execution; PM owns defect triage and sign-off.

deliverables.title: Monitoring & Performance Dashboards
deliverables.description: Defines and delivers operational and business dashboards that monitor platform health, product performance, and key outcomes to support faster, more confident decision-making.
deliverables.aiGeneration:
Agent reads `sprint-status.yaml`, deployment logs, and analytics streams (Layer 1, Git) → generates dashboard configuration for observability tooling. 

**ACx Runtime Indicators:** Dashboards surface the ACx metric suite as primary runtime indicators — **Velocity Multiple** (tracking AI-augmented throughput vs. human baseline), **AI Delivery Rate** (complex interactions resolved without escalation), **Experience Continuity Score** (context carry-across sessions and channels), and **AI Contribution Rate** (share of customer-facing output AI-generated or enhanced). These replace cost-per-contact and ticket-volume dashboards as the primary operational lens. Tier 1–3 Observability (sprint throughput, DORA metrics, system health) runs alongside. Human Tech Lead and PM gate: dashboard acceptance and baseline sign-off before go-live.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Success Metrics & Monitoring Definition**
- Establish KPI thresholds, guardrails & alert boundaries
- Classify metrics by audience (Exec, Product, Ops)
- Design dashboard views to align with decision needs
- Define refresh cadence (real-time, hourly, daily)
**Dashboard Architecture & Data Modeling**
- Map metrics to source tables (warehouse/CDP/backend)
- Design data models optimized for reporting
- Define aggregation logic & dimensional breakdowns
- Structure executive, product, and operational dashboard views (if needed)
- Validate metric consistency with Measurement Plan (307)
**Dashboard Validation & Release Readiness**
- Perform metric reconciliation against source systems
- Validate data latency & refresh performance
- Test stakeholder usability & interpretability
- Prepare launch-ready reporting package
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**BI / Dashboard**
- Executive Outcome Dashboard
- Product & Experience Dashboard
- Operational / Platform Health Dashboard
**Metric Definition Sheet**
- KPI Name
- Definition & Formula
- Source Table
- Refresh Logic
- Owner
**Data Model Layer**
- Curated reporting tables
- Aggregation logic
- KPI transformation layer
- Data quality checks

deliverables.title: Solution Test Plan
deliverables.description: A structured plan that defines what will be tested, how it will be tested, and who is responsible, ensuring the solution meets functional, technical, performance, security, and acceptance requirements before release.

working: Test management tools (e.g., Jira, TestRail, Testmo), spreadsheets, documentation tools

deliverables.aiGeneration: QA Agent harness reads `prd.md`, `epics.md`, and acceptance criteria (Layer 1, Git) → generates comprehensive test case library, edge-case scenarios, and test data specifications. Test plan is committed to Git as a structured artifact alongside the stories it covers. Agent summarizes defect patterns and release readiness signals. Human QA lead reviews test coverage and approves scope before execution.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Define test scope, objectives, and success criteria across functional, integration, performance, security, and acceptance testing
- Design detailed test scenarios, cases, and scripts aligned to requirements and user stories
- Plan test environments, data, tooling, and execution sequencing (including defect tracking and retesting)
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Document**
- Overview
- QA Objectives
- Scope of Testing
- CMS Validation
- Accessibility Testing
- Test Management and Traceability
- Testing Environments
- Smoke Testing
- User Story Validation
- Defect Prioritization
- Release Sign-Off Checklist
- UAT

deliverables.title: Solution Tests
deliverables.description: Execution of test cases and scripts to validate the solution against the test plan.
deliverables.aiGeneration: QA Agent harness executes test scripts against defined environments; automated test frameworks run on every PR via CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions). Test execution logs are committed to Git (Layer 1). Human QA lead reviews defect patterns and approves regression sign-off before release.

deliverables.title: Solution Test Results
deliverables.description: The execution and documented results of planned testing activities, comprising the actual test cases, scripts, runs, defects, and outcomes used to verify that the solution meets defined requirements.

working: Test management tools, automated test frameworks.
internal: Test case repositories, execution logs, defect reports, test results summaries.
external: Test results reports, release readiness summaries (as required).

deliverables.aiGeneration: QA Agent harness generates test execution summaries and defect pattern analysis from test logs committed to Git (Layer 1). Release readiness report is auto-generated from structured test data. Human QA lead and PM review before client-facing release sign-off. Test results reports are Layer 3 generated outputs from the Git source.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**UAT (activity, not deliverable)**
- Develop detailed test cases and scripts aligned to requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria
- Execute test cases across defined test types (e.g., functional, integration, regression, performance, security, UAT support)
- Log, track, and retest defects through resolution
- Validate that entry and exit criteria defined in the Solution - Test Plan are met
- Produce test results summaries and readiness assessments
deliverables.structuralFramework: N/A

deliverables.title: Run Book
deliverables.description: An operational guide that documents how the solution is run, supported, and maintained in production, providing clear, actionable instructions for monitoring, incident response, troubleshooting, deployments, and ongoing operations.

working: Documentation tools (e.g., Confluence, Notion), runbook templates, diagrams.
internal: Operational runbook documents, escalation matrices, on-call guides

deliverables.aiGeneration: Run Book Agent harness reads `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md`, `src/` implementation details, and failure patterns from Git history (Layer 1) → generates `runbook.md` with troubleshooting guides, deployment procedures, and escalation matrices. Human Tech Lead reviews and approves before client handoff. `runbook.md` in Git is the canonical source; Confluence/Notion/PDF are Layer 3 generated outputs. Agent keeps `runbook.md` current as architecture evolves through the build phase.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Document system architecture, environments, and dependencies relevant to operations
- Define monitoring, alerting, and escalation procedures
  Document incident management, troubleshooting steps, and common failure scenarios
- Define deployment, rollback, and release procedures
  Capture operational roles, responsibilities, and support handoff processes
deliverables.structuralFramework: N/A

deliverables.title: Content Model
deliverables.description: The structured schema defining all content types, their fields, relationships, and validation rules within the CMS or headless content platform. In ACx terms, the Content Model is not only a CMS configuration — it is the agent-legible layer through which third-party and brand agents parse, negotiate, and represent the brand in Mode 02 (agent-led) customer interactions. Machine-readable product data, semantic APIs, and structured brand context are the prerequisite for agent-readiness. A content model not designed for agent consumption is invisible to the agents that will increasingly act on the customer's behalf.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Content Model Agent harness reads `requirements/prd.md`, IA structure, wireframe annotations, and PRD requirements (Layer 1, Git) → generates content type definitions, field structures, and CMS-specific model mappings for the target platform (Contentful, Contentstack, etc.). Agent audits proposed model against CMS best practices. Output is `content-model.json` committed to Git (Layer 1). Human Tech Lead and Content Architect review before CMS implementation.
deliverables.keyActivities:
- Review features, stories, information architecture and designs
- Design and document content model that maps to specific implementation details within CMS selected (e.g. Contentful, Contentstack, etc.)
- Identification of content authoring requirements and how model will support these requirements.
deliverables.structuralFramework: - JSON Data Model

deliverables.title: Change Management Enablement
deliverables.description: An action-oriented set of tools, training, and communications that put the change strategy into practice and equip teams to drive and sustain new behaviors and processes.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Change Enablement Agent harness reads `change-strategy.md` (Layer 1, Git) → produces role-based training guide drafts, FAQ libraries, and communications plan variants. Survey data is synthesized by agent into adoption signal summaries. Human Strategy lead reviews training materials and approves comms calendar before activation. Materials are committed to Git as structured content; recordings and external deliverables are Layer 3 generated outputs.
deliverables.keyActivities:
**Develop and deliver training sessions, toolkits, and guides**
- Design role-based learning that translates new processes and tools into day-to-day behaviors.
- Produce assets such as playbooks, job aids, facilitator guides, and manager toolkits to support adoption.
- Mobilize change champions to coach peers, demonstrate new ways of working, and escalate barriers.
**Execute communications cadence and feedback loops**
- Activate the communications plan across defined channels with consistent, audience-specific messaging.
- Gather real-time feedback through surveys, listening sessions, and channels to surface risks.
**Monitor adoption signals and iterate materials**
- Track usage, behavior change, and sentiment data to assess whether the change is landing as intended.
- Refine training content, FAQs, and messaging based on observed gaps and emerging needs.
deliverables.structuralFramework:
**Slide deck**
- **Title & agenda:** Frame the purpose of enablement: translating strategy into day-to-day adoption.
- **Journey so far:** Recap change vision, audiences, and risks defined in the strategy phase.
- **Plan on a page:** Communications, training, and reinforcement roadmap sequenced by release.
- **Learning experience design:** Role-based training paths mapped to specific behaviors and tools. Blend of modalities: instructor-led, self-serve, practice environments, job aids.
- **Comms activation plan:** Message architecture and calendar aligned to releases and milestones. Channels, senders, and feedback mechanisms by audience segment.
- **Adoption measurement:** Definition of leading and lagging indicators for behavior change. Data sources: usage analytics, surveys, performance metrics, qualitative feedback.
- **Enablement roadmap:** Sequenced plan tied to product releases and business milestones.Owners, dependencies, and resourcing requirements.

deliverables.title: CMS Enablement
deliverables.description: Interactive sessions designed to train client editors on how to use the CMS, manage workflows, and maintain site integrity. (includes training, guidelines, training materials, etc.)
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent harness reads `content-model.json` and authoring workflow definitions → generates CMS training tasks, sandbox exercises, and workshop facilitation guides. Human Content Author leads live sessions. Agent-generated materials ensure consistency with the implemented content model.
deliverables.keyActivities: Facilitate a workshop(s) with the client authoring team to take them through CMS 101 training as well as a sandbox environment for them to complete tasks under supervision with a live Q&A.

deliverables.title: CMS Guidelines
deliverables.description: A training document for Content Authors to reference for guidance on how to use the CMS.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent harness generates CMS guidelines documentation from `content-model.json` and authoring workflow definitions (Layer 1, Git). Human Content Author reviews and approves. Word Doc is a Layer 3 generated output; Git is the canonical source.
deliverables.keyActivities: - Create thorough training documentation on how to use the CMS and how to author effectively.

deliverables.title: CMS Training Guides & Walkthroughs
deliverables.description: A series of short, modular screen-recordings that allow new hires or new client authors to learn specific CMS tasks asynchronously.
deliverables.aiGeneration: Agent harness reads `content-model.json` → generates structured training scripts and task lists for screen recording sessions. Human Content Author records walkthroughs using agent-generated scripts. Ensures training materials remain aligned with the implemented content model as it evolves.
deliverables.keyActivities: - Create a series of videos that the client authors can watch post project completion for training purposes.

deliverables.title: Epics
deliverables.description: Structured epics derived from the Technical Architecture Document, defining the high-level units of work for planning and backlog creation.
deliverables.aiGeneration: `/bmad-create-epics-and-stories` reads `prd.md` and `ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md` (Layer 1, Git) → produces `epics.md`: a single structured file containing all epics and user stories with full acceptance criteria, built progressively through 4 steps. `/bmad-sprint-planning` reads `epics.md` → generates `sprint-status.yaml` committed to Git (Layer 1) tracking epic/story status. `/bmad-check-implementation-readiness` validates that the epic set meets definition-of-ready criteria before sprint commitment. Human Product and Tech Lead gate: review and approve epic scope and story readiness before dev handoff.
